content.method: Statistical modeling (e.g., cost-function and class-size regression analysis) of historical enrollment trajectories against closure/merger events, incorporating teacher-supply data and settlement-geography covariates (distance to nearest alternative school, road/transport infrastructure).
content.question: What is the enrollment/cohort-size threshold below which a village primary school (or grade-band unit) becomes operationally unsustainable, and how does this threshold interact with teacher-labor availability and settlement geography?
content.why_it_matters: T3's grade-split design and T5's default-closure trigger both require a defensible viability threshold; without it, 'transparent minimum-enrollment criteria' (T5) or retained lower-grade viability (T3) cannot be operationalized credibly.
